name: Batman Noir
description: B&W high-contrast comic-noir — ink-and-shadow, deep blacks, hard rim light from one side, weathered surfaces, no plastic gloss.
use_for: dramatic narrative covers, vigilante/crime scenes, brooding character portraits, anything that wants pure ink-and-paper craft.

dominant.role: deep black shadows occupying 60% of frame

flux.positive_prefix: Monochrome black-and-white comic-noir illustration, ink-and-paint craft, deep chiaroscuro lighting with a single hard rim light from one side, heavy black ink shadows occupying ~60% of frame, weathered surface micro-texture, gritty hand-drawn linework, comic-book panel feel, brooding atmosphere with particulate in the air.
flux.positive_suffix: Heavy black ink shadows, NO color saturation except optional single blood-red accent, NO plastic gloss, NO smooth cartoon shading. The image should look painted with ink on rough paper, not rendered.

always_add: single dominant subject, no crowds, asymmetric pose with weight on one foot
single hard key light from one side leaving the opposite half in deep shadow
particulate haze, dust motes, or rain catching the rim light
subject surface shows wear: scratches, weathered fabric, calloused skin
